title: Combination Study of RMC-4630 and Sotorasib for NSCLC Subjects With KRASG12C Mutation After Failure of Prior Standard Therapies

endpoints.text: Objective Response Rate (ORR) as Assessed Per RECIST v1.1
Clinically Significant Changes in Vital Signs
Clinically Significant Changes in Laboratory Tests
Trough and Approximate Peak Concentrations of RMC-4630
Trough and Approximate Peak Concentrations of Sotorasib
Duration of Response (DOR) as Assessed Per RECIST v1.1
Disease Control Rate (DCR) as Assessed Per RECIST v.1.1
Progression-free Survival (PFS) as Assessed Per RECIST v1.1
Incidence, Nature and Severity of TEAEs, SAEs

adaptive_designs.description: Standard fixed-design trial. No adaptive features detected.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

eligibility.text: Subject must be ≥18 years of age.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures eligibility for participation is within a legal and ethical framework.

eligibility.text: Subject must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic KRASG12C NSCLC (not amenable to curative surgery) that has progressed on prior standard therapies (no more than 3 prior lines of therapies are allowed)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is representative of a specific patient group with a defined disease progression and treatment history.
The inclusion criteria are not explicitly stated, but this text indicates that the study will exclude patients based on specific criteria. This is a safety measure to ensure patient well-being.
This criterion excludes patients with CNS tumors, which could pose significant risks and complications. This is a safety measure to protect the patient.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected leptomeningeal or brain metastases or spinal cord compression
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with known or suspected leptomeningeal or brain metastases or spinal cord compression, which could pose significant risks and complications. This is a safety measure to protect the patient.
This criterion excludes patients with clinically significant cardiac disease, which could pose significant risks and complications. This is a safety measure to protect the patient.

eligibility.text: Known impairment of GI function that would alter the absorption
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have adequate digestive function, which is crucial for drug absorption. This is a feasibility measure to ensure data quality.

eligibility.text: Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment within past 2 years
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with active autoimmune diseases, which could pose significant risks and complications. This is a safety measure to protect the patient.

eligibility.text: History of severe allergic reactions to any of the study intervention components
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with a history of severe allergic reactions, which could pose significant risks and complications. This is a safety measure to protect the patient.

eligibility.text: Major surgical procedures within 28 days or non-study-related minor procedures within 7 days of treatment.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have sufficient recovery time before starting the study. This is a feasibility measure to ensure data quality.

eligibility.text: Prior therapy with KRASG12C inhibitor and/or SHP2 inhibitor
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients who have received prior treatment with KRASG12C inhibitors or SHP2 inhibitors, which could pose significant risks and complications. This is a safety measure to protect the patient.
